name: Mary Martin
abstract: Mary Virginia Martin (December 1, 1913 – November 3, 1990) was an American actress and singer. A muse of Rodgers and Hammerstein, she originated many leading roles on stage over her career, including Nellie Forbush in South Pacific (1949), the title character in Peter Pan (1954), and Maria von Trapp in The Sound of Music (1959).

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Charlap
about_quotes.evidence: my father was [Mark] Moose Charlap who was a songwriter/composer. The most famous thing he’s known for is the music from “Peter Pan,” — Mary Martin Peter Pan, which he wrote most of the music for
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Charlap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Lee Paige
about_quotes.evidence: Yes, Mary Martin played in the Broadway show, Louisiana Hayride and also Hollywood Follies. And these other people, I worked with Helen Morgan, too...

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: I think I did the Ethel Merman and what's her name, the girl from Texas, Mary Martin, and the Ethel Merman show.

about_quotes.speaker: Ann Rabson
about_quotes.evidence: I just felt like Mary Martin felt about videotaping, that it’s going to take away from live performance.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ann Rabson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 21, 2007
